subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Area & Volume from Region Bounded by Two Curves
stimulus: The shaded region $R$ is bounded by the graphs of the functions $f$ and $g$, where $f(x) = x^2 - 2x$ and $g(x) = x + \sin(\pi x)$, as shown in the figure. The region $R$ is bounded between $x = 0$ and $x = 3$.

parts.prompt: Find the area of $R$. Show the setup for your calculations.
parts.scoring_notes: Area $= \int_0^3 (g(x) - f(x))\, dx \approx 5.137$. Exact answer $= \frac{4+9\pi}{2\pi}$. Accept $\int_0^3 |g(x)-f(x)|\, dx$ or the correct numerical answer. The integrand $f(x) - g(x)$ with negative sign resolved earns both points.

parts.prompt: Region $R$ is the base of a solid. For this solid, at each $x$ the cross section perpendicular to the $x$-axis is a rectangle with height $x$ and base in region $R$. Find the volume of the solid. Show the setup for your calculations.
parts.scoring_notes: Volume $= \int_0^3 x(g(x)-f(x))\, dx \approx 7.705$. Exact answer $= \frac{12+27\pi}{4\pi}$. The integrand must be a product with one factor $= x$ and one factor $= g(x)-f(x)$ or equivalent.

scoring_points.description: Correct integrand as product of two factors, one being $x$

required_elements: Correct integrand as product of two factors, one being $x$

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression for the volume of the solid generated when the region $R$ is rotated about the horizontal line $y = -2$.
parts.scoring_notes: Washer method: $V = \pi\int_0^3\left[(g(x)+2)^2 - (f(x)+2)^2\right]\, dx$. P5 earned for $R^2 - r^2$ form where one of $\{R, r\}$ involves $g(x)$ or $f(x)$ with a nonzero constant. P6 earned for the correct expression. P7 requires $\pi$, correct limits, and $dx$. Reversing the difference with negative sign or reversed limits can still earn P7 if consistent.

scoring_points.description: Correct form of integrand using washer method: outer and inner radii as differences from $y = -2$; $R^2 - r^2$ where $R = g(x) - (-2) = g(x) + 2$ and $r = f(x) - (-2) = f(x) + 2$

required_elements: Correct form of integrand using washer method: outer
inner radii as differences from $y = -2$; $R^2 - r^2$ where $R = g(x) - (-2) = g(x) + 2$

scoring_points.description: Correct constant $\pi$, limits $0$ to $3$, and differential $dx$

parts.prompt: It can be shown that $g'(x) = 1 + \pi\cos(\pi x)$. Find the value of $x$, for $0 < x < 1$, at which the line tangent to the graph of $f$ is parallel to the line tangent to the graph of $g$.
parts.scoring_notes: P8 requires presenting $f'(x) = g'(x)$, or substituting $2x-2$ for $f'(x)$ and/or $1+\pi\cos(\pi x)$ for $g'(x)$. Solve numerically: $x \approx 0.676$. Note $f'(x) = 2x - 2$.
